host: Intel Core Ultra 9 275HX (Arrow Lake-HX, 24 cores; AVX2 + AVX-VNNI + F16C + SHA-NI, no AVX-512)
note: Single-thread micro-benchmarks of the qjl-cpu / polarquant-cpu plugin dispatch paths after adding the runtime AVX-VNNI tier and the Polar pre-Hadamard SIMD variant. Best-of-20 inner-loop wall time. Compiled with -O3 -mavx2 -mfma (-mavxvnni for the VNNI TU).

qjl_int8_score.workload: n_heads=32, n_kv_heads=8, n_tokens=4096 -> 131072 scores; head_dim=128, proj_dim=256
qjl_int8_score.parity: bit-exact integer dot vs qjl_score_qk_i8_ref (max_abs 0.0 over 12*257 outputs in qjl_avxvnni_smoke); ~1.2e-3 abs vs the exact fp32 qjl_score_qk_ref baseline (int8 sketch quantization error, unchanged from the existing reference path)

polar_preht_dot.workload: 32768 Polar K-rows x 128 dims dotted against one query (use_qjl=1)
polar_preht_dot.parity: rel_err < 1e-5 vs ggml_vec_dot_q4_polar_preht_f32_ref over 64 blocks (polar_preht_simd_parity_test), for both use_qjl=0 and use_qjl=1; identical algebra dot(H*x,q)==dot(x,H*q), avoids the per-K-row 7-stage Hadamard and the 128-float decode-to-scratch
